metrics.narrative: No quantified metrics or measurable business impact have been captured anywhere. The only signal is Grace Kim's 16 Jun 2026 Next Step note that Greater Orlando Aviation Authority has 'an active project for Spend Analysis' and 'interest in eProc' -- this names the problem area but carries no numbers on spend volume, cycle time, or cost of the status quo. The one buyer call where this surfaced was never recorded (bot denied entry), so there is no transcript to mine for detail. The 10 Jul 2026 demo (tomorrow) is the next chance to quantify the need.

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. Kelly Loll organized the 16 Jun 2026 intro call and Cheryl Olson also attended, but neither has demonstrated advocacy (pulling in other stakeholders, pushing internally for urgency). If anything, the demo has slipped twice due to a 'GOAA internal conflict' (30 Jun 2026 note), suggesting no one internally is yet driving this forward.

competition.narrative: Amazon Business and Oracle are logged in Salesforce's Competitors__c field, but with zero supporting detail -- no pricing conversation, no stated incumbent, no buyer comparison, since the sole call is unrecorded. Given GOAA is a public aviation authority, Oracle could plausibly represent an existing ERP/procurement system and Amazon Business a marketplace/tail-spend channel, but this is inference, not confirmed evidence.

identify_pain.narrative: Grace Kim's 16 Jun 2026 note records that GOAA has 'an active project for Spend Analysis' and 'interest in eProc,' which names the problem area (spend visibility/analysis and procurement digitization) but not the underlying pain, its owner, or its cost. This is the single strongest MEDDPICC signal on the deal, but it is a rep-logged summary of an unrecorded call, not a buyer quote.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting, vendor-registration, or legal discussion has occurred -- appropriately, since the deal is still pre-discovery. Public-sector buyers like GOAA typically require vendor registration and, above a spend threshold, board approval, but none of this has been surfaced in any call or field yet.

economic_buyer.narrative: Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c are both false in Salesforce, and no Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c or sponsor field is populated. Kelly Loll, Vice President of Procurement Services, organized the 16 Jun 2026 intro call and is the most senior named contact, but her specific role in this purchase (sponsor, evaluator, or approver) has not been confirmed on any recorded conversation. No budget owner or approval chain has been named.

flags: Run full MEDDPICC discovery on 1 Jul: map the Economic Buyer (likely Kelly Loll VP Procurement or Lance Lyttle CEO), confirm the approval chain and budget threshold, surface decision criteria (must-haves, scoring rubric, RFP vs sole-source), quantify the pain (spend leakage, cycle time, manual processes), and ask what happens if they do nothing. Capture answers in writing and log them in Salesforce Business Requirements and Value to Customer fields.

Activate AI differentiation early (1 Jul demo): position Merlin Intake (AI request triage, deflection, NLP) + ML iAnalyze (spend intelligence, supplier risk, contract analytics) as the modern, AI-native stack that beats Amazon Business's shallow marketplace analytics and Oracle's legacy complexity.
